zh.Brixham, Torbay, Devon, England: 英國戴文托爾巴易布利哈姆
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John Cole於1305年出生在英國戴文托爾巴易布利哈姆時，他的父親Sir Rodger Cole II26歲，母親Lady Cecily Weston26歲。他於1330年與Margaret de Felton在英國英格蘭戴文結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1380年在英國享年75歲，埋葬在英國戴文。

zh-hans.Brixham, Torbay, Devon, England: 英国戴文托爾巴易布利哈姆
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir John Cole于1305年出生在英国戴文托爾巴易布利哈姆时，他的父亲Sir Rodger Cole II26岁，母亲Lady Cecily Weston26岁。他于1330年与Margaret de Felton在英国英格兰戴文结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1380年在英国享年75岁，埋葬在英国戴文。

nameMeanings.name: Cole
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: usually from the Middle English and Old French personal name Col(e), Coll(e), Coul(e), a pet form of Nicol (see Nichol and Nicholas ), a common personal name from the mid 13th century onward. English families with this name migrated to Scotland and to Ulster (especially Fermanagh).

English: occasionally perhaps from a different (early) Middle English personal name Col, of native English or Scandinavian origin. Old English Cola was originally a nickname from Old English col ‘coal’ in the sense ‘coal-black (of hair), swarthy’ and is the probable source of most of the examples in Domesday Book. In the northern and eastern counties of England settled by Vikings in the 10th and 11th centuries, alternative sources are Old Norse Kolr and Koli (either from a nickname ‘the swarthy one’ or a short form of names in Kol-), and Old Norse Kollr (from a nickname, perhaps ‘the bald one’).
English: nickname for someone with swarthy skin or black hair, from Middle English col, coul(e) ‘charcoal, coal’ (Old English col).

variants.name: Colton
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Nottinghamshire): habitational name from any of various places called Colton in England. Examples in Norfolk, Staffordshire, and North Yorkshire are from the Old English personal name Cola (or the cognate Old Norse Koli; see Cole 2) + Old English tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’. The place so named in Somerset has as its first element the Old English personal name Cūla (of uncertain origin). The one in Cumbria has a river name apparently derived from a Celtic word meaning ‘hazel’. This English name is also common in Ireland; it was the name of a bishop of Derry in 1397. There seems also to have been confusion with Culliton .
Scottish and Irish (Monaghan): shortened and altered form of Gaelic Mac Haldan, ‘son of Haldan’, see Haldane .

variants.name: Colclough
meanings: English (Staffordshire and Cheshire): habitational name from Cowclough in the parish of Whitworth, Lancashire, earlier recorded as Colleclogh, probably named with the Old English byname Cola (see Cole 2) + Old English clōh ‘ravine’.

variants.name: Calcote
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places (in Bedfordshire, Berkshire, Cambridgeshire, Cheshire, Northamptonshire, Warwickshire, and elsewhere) named Caldecote or Caldecott, from Old English cald ‘cold’ + cot ‘cottage, dwelling’. It has been suggested that in Old English this expression denoted an unattended shelter for wayfarers, although in fact some places with this name were of considerable status by 1086, when they appear in Domesday Book. In some instances this and some of the other shortened forms may have arisen from Calcot in Berkshire, Collacott(s) in Devon, or Calcutt in Wiltshire, in all of which the first element apparently comes from the Old English personal name Cola (see Cole 2) or the word col ‘(char)coal’, in which case the meaning would be something like ‘coalshed’.

variants.name: Collignon
meanings: French and Walloon: from a shortened pet form (see Cole and Colle ) of the French personal name Nicolas (see Nicholas ).

variants.name: Colling
meanings: English (northern England and Devon): from the Old Norse personal name Kollungr, a derivative of Koli, or from Old English Colling, a derivative of Cola (see Cole ). Alternatively, from a pet form of the personal name Col(l), a pet form of Nicholas (see also Colin ).
German: variant of   Kolling   and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.

variants.name: Collard
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Donat, Normand, Octave, Adelard, Aldor, Andre, Armand, Emile, Germaine, Marcel.
English (southern), French, and Walloon: from a diminutive of the Middle English and Old French personal name Col(e), a pet form of Nic(h)olas (see Cole ), formed with the Old French suffix -ard.

variants.name: Coale
meanings: English: post-medieval form of   Cole  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Coles
meanings: English (southern): variant of Cole , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Nichol
meanings: Scottish and northern English: from the Middle English personal name Nic(h)ol(e), a vernacular form of Nic(h)olas (see Nicholas ). Compare Nickel and Nicol .

In some cases also an American shortened and altered form of Greek Nikolas , its variant Nicolas , or of any of various derivatives of the personal name Nikola(o)s ‘Nicholas’, including Nikolis (see Nicoli ). Compare Nicholas and Nicol .

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John Cole was born in 1305, in Brixham, Torbay, Devon, England, his father, Sir Rodger Cole II, was 26 and his mother, Lady Cecily Weston, was 26. He married Margaret de Felton in 1330, in Devon, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in 1380, in England, at the age of 75, and was buried in Devon, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir John Cole est né en 1305, à Brixham, Torbay, Devon, Angleterre, son père, Sir Rodger Cole II, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Lady Cecily Weston, avait 26 ans. Il a épousé Margaret de Felton en 1330, à Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1380, à Angleterre, à l’âge de 75 ans, et a été enterré à Devon, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir John Cole 1305 in Brixham, Torbay, Devon, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Rodger Cole II 26 und seine Mutter Lady Cecily Weston 26. Er hat 1330 in Devon, England, United Kingdom Margaret de Felton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1380 im Alter von 75 Jahren in England und wurde in Devon, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John Cole è nato il nel 1305 nel Brixham, Torbay, Devon, England, il suo padre, Sir Rodger Cole II, aveva 26 anni e il suo madre, Lady Cecily Weston, aveva 26 anni. Lui sposato Margaret de Felton nel 1330, nel Devon, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1380, nel England, all'età di 75 anni, e fu sepolto a Devon, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir John Coleが1305年にイギリスデボントルバイブリクスハムで生まれた時，父Sir Rodger Cole IIは26歳，母Lady Cecily Westonは26歳であった。1330年にイギリスイギリスデボンでMargaret de Feltonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1380年にイギリスで75歳で死去し，イギリスデボンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John Cole(이)가 1305년 영국 데폰 토르베이 브리스함에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Rodger Cole II(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Cecily Weston(은)는 26세였다. 그는 1330년 영국 잉글랜드 데폰에서 Margaret de Felton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1380년 영국에서 75세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 데폰에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John Cole nasceu em 1305, em Brixham, Torbay, Devon, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Rodger Cole II, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Lady Cecily Weston, tinha 26 anos. Ele casou-se com Margaret de Felton em 1330, em Devon, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 1380, em Inglaterra, com 75 anos, e foi sepultado em Devon, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir John Cole родился в 1305 г. в местности бриксхам, торбеи, девон, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Rodger Cole II было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Cecily Weston, 26. Он женился на женщине по имени Margaret de Felton в 1330 г. в местности девон, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в 1380 г. в местности Англия в возрасте 75 лет и был похоронен в местности девон, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir John Cole nació en 1305, en Brixham, Torbay, Devon, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Rodger Cole II, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Lady Cecily Weston, tenía 26 años. Se casó con Margaret de Felton en 1330, en Devon, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1380, en Inglaterra, a la edad de 75 años, y fue sepultado en Devon, Inglaterra.
